data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T134128-cc3a8d26/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T134128-cc3a8d26/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T134128-cc3a8d26/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T134128-cc3a8d26/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T134128-cc3a8d26/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-49d8d6cb">
    <title>agent: introduce attempt-cycle coordinator interfaces</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The current execute-bead implementation conflates worktree lifetime, implementation dispatch, artifact writing, landing, and post-merge review across ExecuteBeadWithConfig and the execute loop. Candidate-cycle review/repair needs a coordinator that owns the full live-worktree lifecycle before final land.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:668-680 documents ExecuteBeadWithConfig as the thin worker and says merge/update-ref/gate/preserve are parent responsibilities.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:765-767 removes the worktree via defer inside ExecuteBeadWithConfig, preventing same-worktree review/repair after the function returns.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:888-900 calls agenttry.Attempt around the executor, then handles review later in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1086-1104.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:146-299 owns generic attempt outcome classification, but not candidate-cycle review/repair.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce narrow interfaces for AttemptCycleCoordinator, ImplementationPass, CandidateRefStore, CandidateCheckRunner, CandidateReviewer, RepairPass, and CandidateLander or equivalent names.
- Split the implementation pass from terminal worktree cleanup: implementation produces a candidate result inside a live worktree; the coordinator owns cleanup after terminal disposition.
- Preserve existing no_changes, no_evidence, and execution_failed behavior while creating seams for candidate checks, review, repair, and final land.
- Add basic cleanup liveness protection for active candidate-cycle worktrees before the deferred removal is moved.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not yet run pre-land review.
- Do not yet implement repair classification or repair loops.
- Do not enable two-slot review.

DEPS
Depends on the spec bead for vocabulary and on the read-only reviewer profile only for interface shape; this bead may define the coordinator before reviewer execution is moved.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. AttemptCycleCoordinator and the pass/check/review/repair/land interfaces (or equivalent names documented in code comments) exist in cli/internal/agent.
2. ExecuteBeadWithConfig or an extracted ImplementationPass can return a live worktree candidate result without owning terminal cleanup.
3. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_SinglePassSuccessStillLands verifies the coordinator can preserve current success behavior without review/repair enabled.
4. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_NoChangesBehaviorUnchanged verifies no_changes adjudication remains unchanged.
5. TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_LiveWorktreeProtected verifies active cycle metadata prevents cleanup from deleting the worktree mid-cycle.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestAttemptCycleCoordinator" green.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, kind:refactor, reliability, adr:024, spec:FEAT-010, candidate-cycle</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-31e07fdc" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="9ceb6abf6450c4730da001c11015d2fb0a4479e3" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T134128-cc3a8d26"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Commit `4743dc8` is done. Here's what was delivered against each AC:

| AC | Evidence |
|----|----------|
| 1 | `AttemptCycleCoordinator`, `ImplementationPass`, `CandidateRefStore`, `CandidateCheckRunner`, `CandidateReviewer`, `RepairPass`, `CandidateLander` defined in `cli/internal/agent/candidate_cycle.go` |
| 2 | `ImplementationPass.Execute` returns `CandidateResult` (with `WorktreePath`) without owning cleanup; coordinator owns disposal |
| 3 | `TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_SinglePassSuccessStillLands` — PASS |
| 4 | `TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_NoChangesBehaviorUnchanged` — PASS |
| 5 | `TestAttemptCycleCoordinator_LiveWorktreeProtected` — PASS; `ActiveCandidateCycle bool` added to `ExecutionCleanupMetadata`; liveness probe returns `"active candidate cycle"` when set |
| 6 | `go test ./internal/agent/... -run TestAttemptCycleCoordinator` — green |
| 7 | `go test ./internal/agent/...` — green (166s, all pass) |
| 8 | `lefthook run pre-commit` — all 12 checks pass |
